content.full_text:
MEMO TO FILE 
October 24, 1975 
RE: 
Telephone inquiry re Section 18202 
FROM: 
Natalie 
FROM: 
Ron Gastelum 
Metropolitan Water District 
Los Angeles 
Quasi-legislative proceeding under Section 82002 
(administrative action) and 2 Cal. Admin. Code 
Section 18202. 
Metropolitan Water District negotiates with 
the California Department of Water Resources 
concerning allocating expenditures on the state 
water contract. 
The amount of water that the District will 
purchase from the state is established under a 
pre-existing contract. 
However, each year the 
District incurs certain expenses in planning, 
engineering, etc., which they are allowed to deduct 
from the purchase price paid to DWR. 
Negotiations 
are carried on between the District and DWR to 
determine how much of a deduction will be allowed. 
Question: 
Are these negotiations a "quasi-
legislative proceeding" and thus subject to Prop. 9? 
Answer: 
In my opinion these negotiations are 
carried on to determine the rights of the respective 
parties under a pre-existing contract, and would not 
be quasi-legislative. 
However, our regulation does 
not specifically address this kind of question .... 
It may come within (a) (1) or (a) (4) of 18202. 
I told him that he can request an opinion if he 
wants a formal answer - or that I will send him an 
'informal written answer if he sends a letter setting 
out more specific facts.

However, our regulation does not specifically address this kind of question .... It may come within (a) (1) or (a) (4) of 18202.
sections.question_synthetic: Whether negotiations between the Metropolitan Water District and California Department of Water Resources regarding allocation of expenditures on a state water contract constitute a quasi-legislative proceeding subject to Proposition 9.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The negotiations are likely not quasi-legislative in nature since they determine rights under a pre-existing contract; however, the regulation does not specifically address this situation and it may fall within certain subsections of Section 18202.
sections.parsing_notes: Format: modern; LLM: Internal memo documenting informal telephone inquiry and staff response; not a formal advice letter format. Question and answer sections are present but in informal memo style. Document appears to be staff note-to-file rather than official FPPC correspondence.

embedding.summary: FPPC staff memo documenting a telephone inquiry from the Metropolitan Water District regarding whether contract negotiations with the California Department of Water Resources constitute a quasi-legislative proceeding under Proposition 9. Staff provided an informal preliminary opinion that the negotiations likely do not qualify as quasi-legislative but noted regulatory ambiguity.
